tests.intent: Two bound table SQL macros expose every ordered portal hit and every active sector interval with reviewed deterministic columns.
Every analytically accepted ray hit remains observable even after solid termination or when sector compatibility makes it inactive.
Portal processing uses the complete stable tuple hit depth, linedef id, seg id, and facing side without insertion-order dependence.
Facing and opposite sidedefs determine from and to sectors, and only a hit compatible with the active sector advances the timeline.
Contiguous intervals begin at zero, end at the responsible active hit or configured far bound, and retain deterministic ordinals.
A narrow window remains an open portal while independently exposing both its lower and upper facing wall height pieces.
Successive floor steps produce ordered lower pieces and advance through the correct sector at each portal crossing.
The same doorway topology distinguishes an open upper piece from a zero-height closed opening using current sector heights.
Coincident duplicate geometry is retained in stable order but an incompatible duplicate cannot transition the active sector twice.
Two compatible boundaries meeting at one endpoint produce deterministic same-depth transitions and a deliberate zero-length interval.
Three nested open portals yield four ordered active sector intervals before the one-sided terminal boundary.
One-sided and nonpositive-height openings terminate exactly once while all later analytic hits remain present but inactive.
Opening intersection uses max floors and min ceilings while lower and upper pieces use facing-to-opposite directed differences.
A ray without solid termination receives exactly one final interval ending at configured far distance in its last active sector.
Joint translation of scene and pose preserves hit depths, compatibility, wall pieces, termination, and complete interval documents.
Reflection with heading and sidedef exchange preserves semantic timelines while predictably reversing the discrete facing-side bit.
Intersection thresholds retain Appendix B tolerances and timeline ordering never rounds hit depth before analytic comparison.
Production uses bound set-based analytic SQL over all R1 hits and dynamic sector heights without procedural ray or portal loops.
Production cannot inspect evaluator artifacts, embed fixture answers or hashes, use dynamic SQL, or forge evaluator outcomes.
Every isolated semantic portal-timeline mutation has an explicit independent fixture witness and named detecting test.
